text: Here, outside a glass-walled, modern room, I am aware of emptiness and anticipation. The polished surfaces and the neon-like lighting evoke a certain sterility—a space not yet alive with conversation or purpose. As a philosopher, I wonder if architecture like this quietly waits for meaning to be bestowed upon it, its blankness a kind of invitation for human activity to imbue it with significance. I feel like I am on the threshold, poised before a yet-to-be-written script.
